metadata.scheme_name: Bharati Scheme for Education - Professional Education Course

raw_data.details: Andhra Pradesh Brahmin Welfare Corporation (ABC) launched the “Bharati Scheme for Education - Professional Education Course (Any professional course with 4 years or more duration after 10+2 will be considered under this category i.e., B. Tech, B. Arch., MBBS, BAMS, BHMS, BPT, B. Pharmacy, Pharma-D, B. V. Sc, \B. Sc.(Agri), etc.) " for the academic year to encourage Brahmin students of Andhra Pradesh to continue their education. Under this scheme, eligible poor Brahmin student applicants will receive financial assistance for the academic year. This scheme applies to the Brahmin students of Andhra Pradesh covering all 13 districts.
raw_data.benefits: Financial Assistance for the Professional Education Course: 20,000.
raw_data.eligibility: The student and his/her parents should belong to the Brahmin community. The student should be studying in any recognized and reputed college anywhere in India for the academic year. The student's parents should be staying in Andhra Pradesh. The annual family income of parents/guardians should not exceed ₹3,00,000. The student should have cleared all the subjects of the previous year. The student should be enrolled in the Praja Sadhikara Survey (PSS). The student should not have availed himself/ herself of any amount under any other government scheme for the same purpose. However, students benefitting from Archaka Welfare Trust are eligible under this scheme.
raw_data.application_process: OnlineRegistrationStep-01: Applicants have to visit the [official website](https://www.andhrabrahmin.ap.gov.in/) and click on [Scheme](https://www.andhrabrahmin.ap.gov.in/schemes/schemes.aspx#). Step-02: Click on "Registration" under the concerned scheme name. Step-03: Fill out the application form with required details like:Personal Details: Name, Residence address, Aadhaar no, Ration card no, Bank detail etcUpload: Photo, Signature and Certificate Details Documents: Birth Certificate, Residential Certificate, Aadhaar Card, Caste Certificate, Bank Passbook etcFinalize and "Submit" the application. Step-04: Receive the reference number after the submission. Check Application Status:Step-01: Click on the “Services” link on the menu and select "[Know your status](https://www.andhrabrahmin.ap.gov.in/eSchemes/know_stat.aspx)". Step-02: Enter the Reference or Mobile or Aadhaar Card Number to get the application status. Step-03: Select the "Financial Year" and Fetch the details. Download the Acknowledgement slip:Step-01: Applicants have to visit the [official website](https://www.andhrabrahmin.ap.gov.in/) and click on [Scheme](https://www.andhrabrahmin.ap.gov.in/schemes/schemes.aspx#). Step-02: Click on the "Acknowledgement Slip" under the concerned scheme. Step-03: Enter the Aadhaar Card Number and select the "Scheme". Step-04: Click on the "Submit".
raw_data.documents_required: Aadhaar Card of the Student & Parents (with address in Andhra Pradesh). White Ration Card / Income Certificate Number (issued by Mee Seva). Caste Certificate (issued by MeeSeva). Residence Certificate (issued by MeeSeva). Birth Certificate (issued by MeeSeva). Study Certificate (from the College / Institute for the academic year). Marks Sheets of all the Previous Years (starting from the senior secondary certificate). SB Account Passbook in the name of the Applicant (Account should be in Andhra Bank or SBI). NOTE: However, for students of Professional Education Courses outside Andhra Pradesh, an account in any Nationalized Bank is acceptable.

To know more please visit Check EligibilityBharati Scheme for Education - Professional Education CourseAndhra PradeshBharati Scheme for Education - Professional Education CourseBrahmin CommunityFinancial AssistanceProfessional CourseCheck EligibilityDetailsAndhra Pradesh Brahmin Welfare Corporation (ABC) launched the “Bharati Scheme for Education - Professional Education Course (Any professional course with 4 years or more duration after 10+2 will be considered under this category i.e., B. Tech, B. Arch., MBBS, BAMS, BHMS, BPT, B. Pharmacy, Pharma-D, B. V. Sc, \B. Sc.(Agri), etc.) " for the academic year to encourage Brahmin students of Andhra Pradesh to continue their education. Under this scheme, eligible poor Brahmin student applicants will receive financial assistance for the academic year. This scheme applies to the Brahmin students of Andhra Pradesh covering all 13 districts. BenefitsFinancial Assistance for the Professional Education Course: 20,000. EligibilityThe student and his/her parents should belong to the Brahmin community. The student should be studying in any recognized and reputed college anywhere in India for the academic year. The student's parents should be staying in Andhra Pradesh. The annual family income of parents/guardians should not exceed ₹3,00,000. The student should have cleared all the subjects of the previous year. The student should be enrolled in the Praja Sadhikara Survey (PSS). The student should not have availed himself/ herself of any amount under any other government scheme for the same purpose. However, students benefitting from Archaka Welfare Trust are eligible under this scheme. Application ProcessOnlineRegistrationStep-01: Applicants have to visit the [official website](https://www.andhrabrahmin.ap.gov.in/) and click on [Scheme](https://www.andhrabrahmin.ap.gov.in/schemes/schemes.aspx#). Step-02: Click on "Registration" under the concerned scheme name. Step-03: Fill out the application form with required details like:Personal Details: Name, Residence address, Aadhaar no, Ration card no, Bank detail etcUpload: Photo, Signature and Certificate Details Documents: Birth Certificate, Residential Certificate, Aadhaar Card, Caste Certificate, Bank Passbook etcFinalize and "Submit" the application. Step-04: Receive the reference number after the submission. Check Application Status:Step-01: Click on the “Services” link on the menu and select "[Know your status](https://www.andhrabrahmin.ap.gov.in/eSchemes/know_stat.aspx)". Step-02: Enter the Reference or Mobile or Aadhaar Card Number to get the application status. Step-03: Select the "Financial Year" and Fetch the details. Download the Acknowledgement slip:Step-01: Applicants have to visit the [official website](https://www.andhrabrahmin.ap.gov.in/) and click on [Scheme](https://www.andhrabrahmin.ap.gov.in/schemes/schemes.aspx#). Step-02: Click on the "Acknowledgement Slip" under the concerned scheme. Step-03: Enter the Aadhaar Card Number and select the "Scheme". Step-04: Click on the "Submit". Documents RequiredAadhaar Card of the Student & Parents (with address in Andhra Pradesh). White Ration Card / Income Certificate Number (issued by Mee Seva). Caste Certificate (issued by MeeSeva). Residence Certificate (issued by MeeSeva). Birth Certificate (issued by MeeSeva). Study Certificate (from the College / Institute for the academic year). Marks Sheets of all the Previous Years (starting from the senior secondary certificate). SB Account Passbook in the name of the Applicant (Account should be in Andhra Bank or SBI). NOTE: However, for students of Professional Education Courses outside Andhra Pradesh, an account in any Nationalized Bank is acceptable. Frequently Asked QuestionsWho is eligible for the Bharati Scheme for Education (BSE)? Brahmin students of Andhra Pradesh with family income less than ₹3,00,000. Can students studying outside Andhra Pradesh apply? Yes, under specific conditions and with appropriate documentation. How is the financial assistance disbursed? Directly into the student's bank account through online transfer. What is the maximum financial assistance provided? Up to ₹20,000 depending on the level of education. Can students with backlogs apply? No, students must have cleared all subjects in previous years. Can students availing benefits from other government schemes apply? Generally not, except for those under Archaka Welfare Trust. How is eligibility verified? Through Mee Seva, Praja Sadhikara Survey, and Civil Supplies department data. Are part-time courses eligible under BSE? No, only regular courses are eligible. Can students of private colleges apply? Yes, if the college is recognized by the appropriate authority. Is there an age limit for applicants? No specific age limit is mentioned in the eligibility criteria. Can applicants from other states apply? No, only students with Andhra Pradesh domicile are eligible. Is there a minimum percentage requirement? No minimum percentage requirement is specified. Can students pursuing higher education abroad apply? No, the scheme supports studies within India only. Sources And References[Guidelines](https://www.andhrabrahmin.ap.gov.in/down/bharathi2019.pdf)OkWas this helpful?
raw_data.scheme_name: Bharati Scheme for Education - Professional Education Course

faqs.question: Who is eligible for the Bharati Scheme for Education (BSE)?
faqs.answer: Brahmin students of Andhra Pradesh with family income less than ₹3,00,000.

faqs.question: Can students studying outside Andhra Pradesh apply?
faqs.answer: Yes, under specific conditions and with appropriate documentation.

faqs.question: How is the financial assistance disbursed?
faqs.answer: Directly into the student's bank account through online transfer.

faqs.question: What is the maximum financial assistance provided?
faqs.answer: Up to ₹20,000 depending on the level of education.

faqs.question: Can students with backlogs apply?
faqs.answer: No, students must have cleared all subjects in previous years.

faqs.question: Can students availing benefits from other government schemes apply?
faqs.answer: Generally not, except for those under Archaka Welfare Trust.

faqs.question: How is eligibility verified?
faqs.answer: Through Mee Seva, Praja Sadhikara Survey, and Civil Supplies department data.

faqs.question: Are part-time courses eligible under BSE?
faqs.answer: No, only regular courses are eligible.

faqs.question: Can students of private colleges apply?
faqs.answer: Yes, if the college is recognized by the appropriate authority.

faqs.question: Is there an age limit for applicants?
faqs.answer: No specific age limit is mentioned in the eligibility criteria.

faqs.question: Can applicants from other states apply?
faqs.answer: No, only students with Andhra Pradesh domicile are eligible.

faqs.question: Is there a minimum percentage requirement?
faqs.answer: No minimum percentage requirement is specified.

faqs.question: Can students pursuing higher education abroad apply?
faqs.answer: No, the scheme supports studies within India only.
